main_event: Egg-shaped blue iridescent fireball with red tail descended east to west over Southern California in 2 seconds, seen from Port Hueneme, Point Mugu, and Santa Barbara.
sighted_object: egg shaped size of football, blue irredesent color, long firey red tail breaking up, tail had white center, parts braking off
witness_description: Civilians at Port Hueneme, Point Mugu, Santa Barbara, Camarillo; LCDR J.M. Suddreth, USN, pilot, noted as very reliable; O.O.D. NAS Pt Mugu
interesting_points: Egg shaped, size of football, blue irredesent color, parts braking off with long red fiery tail with white center
Descending fm 60° elev in E to horizon in W, duration 2 sec
LCDR Suddreth, USN, pilot, noted as very reliable observer
O.O.D. NAS Pt Mugu: probably a meteor; observer stated it definitely was not an acft flare
Seen for 600 miles in California and at Las Vegas, Nevada, 300 miles inland
Many reported object appeared to be climbing in sky instead of burning itself out
Inquiries from frightened citizens swamped police and newspaper switchboards throughout the area
